title: Leionema rotundifolium
taxonomy: Leionema rotundifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Leionema, which is a group of shrubs native to Australia. Within the genus, Leionema rotundifolium is specifically classified as Leionema rotundifolium. The plant is further classified within the kingdom Plantae, and the kingdom is divided into several subgroups including the subkingdom Angiospermophyta. This classification indicates that Leionema rotundifolium is a type of flowering plant that produces seeds enclosed in a fruit.

intro: Leionema rotundifolium, commonly known as the round-leaved leionema, is a small shrub native to eastern Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the relief of digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Leionema rotundifolium include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Leionema rotundifolium is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including respiratory issues, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems.
